nodes.title: Pyrrhus
nodes.snippet: what city sent for him? / Tarentum / And he said: Another victory like that and we are done for.

nodes.title: Daughters of memory
nodes.snippet: Fabled by the daughters of memory. / thud of Blake's wings of excess. I hear the ruin of all space, / and time one livid final flame.

nodes.title: Haines's chapbook
nodes.snippet: For Haines's chapbook. No-one here to hear. Tonight deftly amid wild drink and talk, to pierce the polished mail of his mind. What then? A jester at the court of his master, indulged and disesteemed, winning a clement master's praise. Why had they chosen all that part? Not wholly for the smooth caress. For them too history was a tale like any other too often heard, their land a pawnshop.

nodes.title: Julius Caesar
nodes.snippet: or Julius Caesar not been knifed to death

nodes.title: Infinite possibilities
nodes.snippet: the infinite possibilities they have ousted / a movement then, an actuality of the possible as possible.

nodes.title: Weave the wind
nodes.snippet: weaver of the wind / woven on the church's looms

nodes.title: Ghoststory
nodes.snippet: ghoststory / The cock crew, / The sky was blue:

nodes.title: Lycidas
nodes.snippet: Weep no more, woful shepherds, weep no more / For Lycidas, your sorrow, is not dead, / Sunk though he be beneath the watery floor...

nodes.title: Siamese
nodes.snippet: a delicate Siamese conned a handbook of strategy

nodes.title: Darkness
nodes.snippet: in my mind's darkness a sloth of the underworld, reluctant, shy of brightness, shifting her dragon scaly folds. / darkness shining in brightness / dark palaces of both

nodes.title: Candescent
nodes.snippet: Thought is the thought of thought. Tranquil brightness. The soul is in a manner all that is: the soul is the form of forms. Tranquility sudden, vast, candescent: form of forms.

nodes.title: Coin of tribute
nodes.snippet: a coin of the tribute. To Caesar what is Caesar's, to / God what is God's.

nodes.title: Riddle me
nodes.snippet: Riddle me, riddle me, randy ro. / My father gave me seeds to sow.

nodes.title: Grandmother
nodes.snippet: The fox burying his grandmother under a hollybush.

nodes.title: Squashed snail
nodes.snippet: trampled him underfoot, a squashed boneless snail

nodes.title: Amor matris
nodes.snippet: Was that then real? The only true thing in life? / Amor matris: subjective and objective genitive.

nodes.title: Hear him on Hamlet
nodes.snippet: He proves by algebra that Shakespeare's ghost is Hamlet's grandfather.

nodes.title: Morrice
nodes.snippet: the symbols moved in grave morrice, in the mummery of their letters, wearing quaint caps of squares and cubes.

nodes.title: And ever shall be
nodes.snippet: As it was in the beginning, / Three times now. Three nooses round me here.

nodes.title: Stuart coins
nodes.snippet: tray of Stuart coins, base treasure of a bog

nodes.title: Hollow shells
nodes.snippet: the shells heaped in the cold stone mortar: whelks and / money cowries and leopard shells: and this, whorled as an emir's turban, and this, the scallop of saint James. An old pilgrim's hoard

nodes.title: Priest spells poverty
nodes.snippet: Because you don't save, Mr Deasy said, pointing his finger. You don't know yet what money is. Money is power.

nodes.title: He made money
nodes.snippet: If youth but knew. But what does Shakespeare say? Put but money in thy purse. / — Iago, Stephen murmured. / He lifted his gaze from the idle shells to the old man's stare.

nodes.title: Owe nothing
nodes.snippet: Can you feel that? I owe nothing. Can you?

nodes.title: Arthur Griffith
nodes.snippet: I fear those big words, Stephen said, which make us so unhappy

nodes.title: Prelates
nodes.snippet: the prelates of your communion denounced him

nodes.title: Corpses of papishes
nodes.snippet: lodge of Diamond in Armagh the splendid behung with corpses of papishes

nodes.title: Sir John Blackwood
nodes.snippet: sir John Blackwood who voted for the union / Per vias rectas, Mr Deasy said firmly, was his motto. He voted for it and put on his topboots to ride to Dublin from the Ards of Down to do so.

nodes.title: Vanished horses
nodes.snippet: Stephen seated himself noiselessly before the princely presence. Framed around the walls images of vanished horses stood in homage, their / meek heads poised in air: lord Hastings' Repulse, the duke of Westminster's Shotover, the duke of Beaufort's Ceylon, prix de Paris, 1866. Elfin riders sat them, watchful of a sign. He saw their speeds, backing king's colours, and shouted with the shouts of vanished crowds.

nodes.title: Even money
nodes.snippet: to get rich quick, hunting his winners among the mudsplashed brakes, amid bawls of bookies on their pitches and reek of the canteen, over the motley slush. Even money Fair Rebel: ten to one the field. Dicers and thimbleriggers we hurried by after the hoofs, the vying caps and jackets and past the meatfaced woman, a butcher's dame, nuzzling thirstily her clove of orange.

nodes.title: Battling bodies
nodes.snippet: Jousts, slush and uproar of battles, the frozen deathspew of the slain, a shout of spearspikes baited with men's bloodied guts.

nodes.title: Into a nutshell
nodes.snippet: a nutshell / I don't mince words, do I?

nodes.title: Foot and mouth disease
nodes.snippet: foot and mouth disease / embargo on Irish cattle

nodes.title: Pluterperfect imperturbability
nodes.snippet: That doctrine of laissez faire which so often in our history. Our cattle trade. The way of all our old industries. Liverpool ring which jockeyed the Galway harbour scheme. European conflagration. Grain supplies through the narrow waters of / the channel. The pluterperfect imperturbability of the department of agriculture. / the department.

nodes.title: Cassandra
nodes.snippet: Cassandra. By a woman who was no better than she should be.

nodes.title: Serum and virus
nodes.snippet: Foot and mouth disease. Known as Koch's preparation. Serum and virus. Percentage of salted horses. Rinderpest. Emperor's horses at Murzsteg, lower Austria. Veterinary surgeons. / regularly treated and cured in Austria by cattledoctors

nodes.title: German Jews
nodes.snippet: England is in the hands of the jews. In all the highest places: her finance, her press. And they are the signs of a nation's decay. Wherever they gather they eat up the nation's vital strength.

nodes.title: Jew merchants
nodes.snippet: jew merchants / They sinned against the light

nodes.title: Old England
nodes.snippet: The harlot's cry from street to street / Shall weave old England's windingsheet.

nodes.title: Wandering Jew
nodes.snippet: wanderers on the earth to this day.

nodes.title: Paris stock exchange
nodes.snippet: On the steps of the Paris stock exchange

nodes.title: History is a nightmare
nodes.snippet: a nightmare from which I am trying to awake.

nodes.title: One great goal
nodes.snippet: All human history moves towards one great goal, the manifestation of God.

nodes.title: Woman brought sin
nodes.snippet: A woman brought sin into the world. For a woman who was no better than she should be, Helen, the runaway wife of Menelaus, ten years the Greeks made war on Troy. A faithless wife first brought the strangers / to our shore here, MacMurrough's wife and her leman, O'Rourke, prince of Breffni. A woman too brought Parnell low.

nodes.title: Ulster will fight
nodes.snippet: For Ulster will fight / And Ulster will be right.

nodes.title: Gravel path
nodes.snippet: down the gravel path under the trees, hearing the cries of voices and crack of sticks from the playfield. The lions couchant on the pillars

nodes.title: She never let them in
nodes.snippet: the only country which never persecuted the jews. / she never let them in
